question:
Problem B-I
Find
\[
\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty}\left[\frac{1}{n^{5}} \sum_{n=1}^{n} \sum_{k=1}^{n}\left(5 h^{4}-18 h^{2} k^{2}+5 k^{4}\right)\right] .
\]
solution:
B-1.
Let \( S_{k}(n)=1^{k}+2^{k}+\cdots+n^{k} \). Using standard methods of calculus texts one finds that
\[
S_{2}(n)=\left(n^{3} / 3\right)+\left(n^{2} / 2\right)+a n
\]
and
\[
S_{4}(n)=\left(n^{5} / 5\right)+\left(n^{4} / 2\right)+b n^{3}+c n^{2}+d n
\]
with \( a, b, c, d \) constants. Then the double sum is
\[
10 n S_{4}(n)-18\left[S_{2}(n)\right]^{2}=\left(2 n^{6}+5 n^{5}+\cdots\right)-\left(2 n^{6}+6 n^{5}+\cdots\right)=-n^{5}+\cdots
\]
and the desired limit is -1 .

descriptive_long.question:
Problem B-I
Find
\[
\lim _{indexvar \rightarrow \infty}\left[\frac{1}{indexvar^{5}} \sum_{indexvar=1}^{indexvar} \sum_{loopvar=1}^{indexvar}\left(5 stepvar^{4}-18 stepvar^{2} loopvar^{2}+5 loopvar^{4}\right)\right] .
\]
descriptive_long.solution:
B-1.
Let \( powersumvar(indexvar)=1^{loopvar}+2^{loopvar}+\cdots+indexvar^{loopvar} \). Using standard methods of calculus texts one finds that
\[
powersumtwo(indexvar)=\left(indexvar^{3} / 3\right)+\left(indexvar^{2} / 2\right)+ coeffone\, indexvar
\]
and
\[
powersumfour(indexvar)=\left(indexvar^{5} / 5\right)+\left(indexvar^{4} / 2\right)+ coefftwo\, indexvar^{3}+ coeffthree\, indexvar^{2}+ coefffour\, indexvar
\]
with \( coeffone, coefftwo, coeffthree, coefffour \) constants. Then the double sum is
\[
10\, indexvar\, powersumfour(indexvar)-18\left[powersumtwo(indexvar)\right]^{2}=\left(2 indexvar^{6}+5 indexvar^{5}+\cdots\right)-\left(2 indexvar^{6}+6 indexvar^{5}+\cdots\right)=-indexvar^{5}+\cdots
\]
and the desired limit is -1 .

descriptive_long_confusing.question:
Problem B-I
Find
\[
\lim _{vineyard \rightarrow \infty}\left[\frac{1}{vineyard^{5}} \sum_{vineyard=1}^{vineyard} \sum_{sailboat=1}^{vineyard}\left(5 drumstick^{4}-18 drumstick^{2} sailboat^{2}+5 sailboat^{4}\right)\right] .
\]
descriptive_long_confusing.solution:
B-1.
Let \( snowflake(vineyard)=1^{sailboat}+2^{sailboat}+\cdots+vineyard^{sailboat} \). Using standard methods of calculus texts one finds that
\[
toothpaste(vineyard)=\left(vineyard^{3} / 3\right)+\left(vineyard^{2} / 2\right)+handgrip vineyard
\]
and
\[
rainstorm(vineyard)=\left(vineyard^{5} / 5\right)+\left(vineyard^{4} / 2\right)+wetsuits vineyard^{3}+marshland vineyard^{2}+paperback vineyard
\]
with \( handgrip, wetsuits, marshland, paperback \) constants. Then the double sum is
\[
10 vineyard rainstorm(vineyard)-18\left[toothpaste(vineyard)\right]^{2}=\left(2 vineyard^{6}+5 vineyard^{5}+\cdots\right)-\left(2 vineyard^{6}+6 vineyard^{5}+\cdots\right)=-vineyard^{5}+\cdots
\]
and the desired limit is -1 .

descriptive_long_misleading.question:
Problem B-I
Find
\[
\lim _{endlesscount \rightarrow \infty}\left[\frac{1}{endlesscount^{5}} \sum_{endlesscount=1}^{endlesscount} \sum_{fixedindex=1}^{endlesscount}\left(5 staticsymbol^{4}-18 staticsymbol^{2} fixedindex^{2}+5 fixedindex^{4}\right)\right] .
\]
descriptive_long_misleading.solution:
B-1.
Let \( differencek(endlesscount)=1^{fixedindex}+2^{fixedindex}+\cdots+endlesscount^{fixedindex} \). Using standard methods of calculus texts one finds that
\[
differencetwo(endlesscount)=\left(endlesscount^{3} / 3\right)+\left(endlesscount^{2} / 2\right)+variableone\, endlesscount
\]
and
\[
differencefour(endlesscount)=\left(endlesscount^{5} / 5\right)+\left(endlesscount^{4} / 2\right)+variabletwo\, endlesscount^{3}+variablethree\, endlesscount^{2}+variablefour\, endlesscount
\]
with \( variableone, variabletwo, variablethree, variablefour \) constants. Then the double sum is
\[
10\, endlesscount\, differencefour(endlesscount)-18\left[differencetwo(endlesscount)\right]^{2}=\left(2 endlesscount^{6}+5 endlesscount^{5}+\cdots\right)-\left(2 endlesscount^{6}+6 endlesscount^{5}+\cdots\right)=-endlesscount^{5}+\cdots
\]
and the desired limit is -1 .

garbled_string.question:
Problem B-I
Find
\[
\lim _{wqxvuhtr \rightarrow \infty}\left[\frac{1}{wqxvuhtr^{5}} \sum_{wqxvuhtr=1}^{wqxvuhtr} \sum_{hdysmpqa=1}^{wqxvuhtr}\left(5 ukgvlecz^{4}-18 ukgvlecz^{2} hdysmpqa^{2}+5 hdysmpqa^{4}\right)\right] .
\]
garbled_string.solution:
B-1.
Let \( zprsagwy(wqxvuhtr)=1^{hdysmpqa}+2^{hdysmpqa}+\cdots+wqxvuhtr^{hdysmpqa} \). Using standard methods of calculus texts one finds that

\[
dcxbnvoj(wqxvuhtr)=\left(wqxvuhtr^{3} / 3\right)+\left(wqxvuhtr^{2} / 2\right)+wtfoesiq \, wqxvuhtr
\]

and
\[
lejmhzid(wqxvuhtr)=\left(wqxvuhtr^{5} / 5\right)+\left(wqxvuhtr^{4} / 2\right)+glrahxcu \, wqxvuhtr^{3}+vuktebjo \, wqxvuhtr^{2}+lyswqamp \, wqxvuhtr
\]
with \( wtfoesiq, glrahxcu, vuktebjo, lyswqamp \) constants. Then the double sum is
\[
10 wqxvuhtr \, lejmhzid(wqxvuhtr)-18\left[dcxbnvoj(wqxvuhtr)\right]^{2}=\left(2 wqxvuhtr^{6}+5 wqxvuhtr^{5}+\cdots\right)-\left(2 wqxvuhtr^{6}+6 wqxvuhtr^{5}+\cdots\right)=-wqxvuhtr^{5}+\cdots
\]
and the desired limit is -1 .

kernel_variant.question:
Let
\[
T_n\;=\;\frac1{n^{7}}\sum_{h=1}^{n}\sum_{k=1}^{n}\left(7h^{6}-32h^{3}k^{3}+7k^{6}\right).
\]
Evaluate \(\displaystyle\lim_{n\to\infty}T_n\).
kernel_variant.solution:
Write the power-sums
S_r(n)=\sum _{m=1}^n m^r   (r\in \mathbb{N}).

1.  Rewrite the double sum.
   Because the summand is symmetric in h and k,
   \sum _{h,k}(7h^6 - 32h^3k^3 + 7k^6)
     =7nS_6(n) + 7nS_6(n) - 32[ S_3(n) ]^2
     =14nS_6(n) - 32[ S_3(n) ]^2.   (1)

2.  Insert Faulhaber expansions (e.g. via Euler-Maclaurin):
   S_6(n)=n^7/7 + n^6/2 + O(n^5),    S_3(n)=n^4/4 + n^3/2 + O(n^2).   (2)

3.  Keep two highest orders and note the leading cancellation.
   Using (2) in (1),
   14nS_6(n) = 14n( n^7/7 + n^6/2 + O(n^5) ) = 2n^8 + 7n^7 + O(n^6),
   -32[ S_3(n) ]^2 = -32( n^8/16 + n^7/4 + O(n^6) ) = -2n^8 - 8n^7 + O(n^6).
   The 2n^8 terms cancel, leaving -n^7 + O(n^6).

4.  Divide by n^7:
   T_n = (-n^7 + O(n^6)) / n^7 = -1 + O(1/n).

5.  Letting n\to \infty  gives
   lim_n\to \infty  T_n = -1.

Therefore, the limit is -1.

core_steps: Rewrite the double sum with power–sums  S_r(n)=∑_{m=1}^n m^r
Apply Faulhaber (asymptotic) formulas for the needed S_r(n)
Keep the two highest orders, observe cancellation of the top order
Divide the result by n^5 to isolate the surviving coefficient
Take n→∞ to obtain the constant limit

slot1.description: coefficient of the h^4 term in the summand

slot2.description: coefficient of the k^4 term in the summand

slot3.description: coefficient of the h^2 k^2 cross term in the summand

slot4.description: exponent on h in the first (pure-h) power, h^{…}

slot5.description: exponent on k in the last (pure-k) power, k^{…}

slot6.description: exponent on both variables in the cross term, h^{…}k^{…}

slot7.description: power of n in the normalizing factor 1/n^{…}
